Ancients
INDO-SCYTHIANS. Aspavarma (ca. AD 20-46). BI tetradrachm (21mm, 9.59 gm, 11h). NGC Choice VF 5/5 - 3/5. Pushkalavati. BAΣIΛEΩΣ BAΣIΛEΩN MEΓAΛOY AZOY, Azes, in military dress, whip upward in right hand, advancing right on horseback, Kharosthi letter to right / Imtravarmaputrasa Aspavarmasa Strategasa Jayatasa (Kharosthi), Athena standing right, transverse spear and shield in left hand, right hand raised in benediction; star above nandipada to left, monogram to right. Senior 183. From the JGC Collection. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

Baktrian Kingdom, Demetrios II, Tetradrachm, ca. 175-170 BC, Silver, NGC

Baktrian Kingdom, Demetrios II, Tetradrachm, ca. 175-170 BC, Silver, NGC
Bust of Demetrios II, diademed and draped, right, all within bead-and-reel border., Athena standing facing, holding spear and shield set on ground; monogram under the shaft., Graded NGC Ch AU★ 55-58, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 5/5 - die shift. Mint State! Slight die shift on reverse. Magnificent golden iridescent toning.
We note the unusual position of the monogram (consisting of a Π and an Α), positioned under Athena's spear, as if it were a pedestal on which it was placed. On other types, it is always found next to the shaft or under the exergue line.
Demetrios II's reign is largely unknown and unclear; he was probably king of the Bactrian kingdom between 175 and 170 BC (although uncertainty remains and reign dates between 150 and 140 BC are sometimes used).
BIGR Demetrius II 1.6 ; Bopearachchi 1E ; Mitchiner 101f ; HGC 12, 126., BAΣIΛEΩΣ ΔHMHTPIOY
DENOMINATION:Tetradrachm
YEAR:ca. 175-170 BC

Royaume de Bactriane, Démetrios II, Tétradrachme, ca. 175-170 BC, Argent, NGC
Buste de Démétrios II, diadémé et drapé, à droite, le tout entouré d'une bordure de perles et de rouleaux., Athéna debout, de face, tenant une lance et un bouclier posé sur le sol ; monogramme sous la hampe., Gradée NGC Ch AU★ 55-58, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 5/5 - die shift. Exemplaire absolument exceptionnel, dans son état de frappe. Léger tréflage au revers. Recouvert d'une somptueuse patine irisée aux reflets dorés. On remarque la position inhabituelle du monogramme (consistant en un Π et un Α), se trouvant sous la lance d'Athéna, à la manière d'un socle sur lequel elle serait posée. Sur les autres types, celui-ci est toujours rencontré à côté de la hampe ou sous la ligne d'exergue.
Le règne de Démetrios II est mal connu, il est à la tête du royaume de Bactriane probablement entre 175 et 170 av. J.-C. (bien qu'une incertitude demeure et que des dates de règne entre 150 et 140 av. J.-C. sont parfois utilisées).
BIGR Demetrius II 1.6 ; Bopearachchi 1E ; Mitchiner 101f ; HGC 12, 126., BAΣIΛEΩΣ / ΔHMHTPIOY

MACEDONIAN KINGDOM. Antigonus II Gonatas (277/6-239 BC). AR tetradrachm (28mm, 17.02 gm, 5h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 2/5. Pella. Horned head of Pan left, lagobolon behind, in tondo of Macedonian shield / BAΣIΛEΩΣ / ANTIΓONOY, Athena advancing left, shield decorated with aegis on left arm, brandishing thunderbolt in right hand; MEP monogram in inner right field. HGC 3.1-, cf. 1042 for similar type. SNG Copenhagen-, cf. 1199-1202 for similar type. Very rare variation with only one other example found in sales archives. Bright flashes of aqua and amber toning. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

SELEUCID KINGDOM. Antiochus IX Eusebes Philopator (Cyzicenus) (114-95 BC). AR tetradrachm (30mm, 16.52 gm, 11h). NGC MS S 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style. Antioch on the Orontes, first reign, spring/summer 113 BC-spring/summer 112 BC. Diademed head of Antiochus IX right with short, curly beard; bead-and-reel border / BAΣIΛEΩΣ / ANTIOXOY | ΦΙΛΟ-ΠΑΤΟΡΟΣ, Athena standing facing, head left, resting left hand on grounded shield, spear resting against arm, Nike in right hand facing left, crowning wreath border; NT monogram above A in outer left field, A in inner right field. SC 2363.a. Wonderfully struck with water-like luster. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
